operation_candidates.name: Diagnose vLLM CUDA OOM at startup
operation_candidates.trigger: Log shows 'torch.OutOfMemoryError: CUDA out of memory' during engine init or warmup.
action: Lower --max-model-len to the realistic max (prompt + output), not the model's architectural max.
Lower --gpu-memory-utilization to 0.85 if other processes share the GPU; raise to 0.95 if vLLM is alone and KV cache is too small.
Add --kv-cache-dtype fp8 (Ampere+) or --enforce-eager (skip CUDA-graph reservation).
Reduce --max-num-seqs (e.g. 256 -> 64 -> 16).
If still OOM at TP=1: bump --tensor-parallel-size to shard the model.
operation_candidates.output: Engine starts; KV cache occupancy logged at boot is non-zero and <100%.

operation_candidates.name: Pick quantization for an open-weights model
operation_candidates.trigger: Deploying a model that doesn't fit at BF16, or where cost/throughput matters more than peak quality.
action: Hopper/Ada/Blackwell GPU available? -> prefer FP8 (effectively lossless across model scales).
No FP8 hardware, need 4x compression? -> AWQ-4 (better 4-bit stability) or GPTQ-4 (slightly better on code tasks).
Avoid INT3 / 3-bit: ~6-point average drop.
Verify on held-out eval set in your domain before shipping.
operation_candidates.output: Chosen --quantization {fp8|awq|gptq|...} flag; documented expected accuracy delta.

operation_candidates.name: Configure tensor/pipeline parallelism
operation_candidates.trigger: Model too large for one GPU; multi-GPU or multi-node deployment.
action: One node + NVLink: --tensor-parallel-size=<GPUs in node>, PP=1.
One node + only PCIe (e.g. L40S, consumer cards): consider --pipeline-parallel-size instead of large TP.
Multi-node: TP=GPUs/node, PP=#nodes. Use InfiniBand if possible.
MoE (Mixtral / DeepSeek-V3): combine DP attention + EP/TP on MoE layers.
Test TP=2 vs TP=4 vs 2x replicas of TP=2 - replicas often win on throughput-per-dollar.
operation_candidates.output: Chosen TP/PP/DP/EP config with measured tokens/s on real workload.

operation_candidates.name: Enable prefix caching
operation_candidates.trigger: Workload has repeated prefixes (shared system prompt, few-shot examples, RAG with stable instructions, multi-turn chat with same persona).
action: Launch with --enable-prefix-caching (V1 often default-on).
Verify cache hit rate via metrics.
For multi-replica clusters, pair with prefix-aware routing.
For multi-tenant, enable cache salting to prevent cross-tenant prefix-hit leakage.
operation_candidates.output: Prefill TTFT drops on cache-hit requests; KV occupancy may rise (cached blocks held longer).
caveats: APC accelerates prefill ONLY; if outputs are long and prefixes don't repeat, gain ~ 0.
Naive round-robin load balancers destroy cache locality.

operation_candidates.name: Triage live performance (TTFT vs ITL)
operation_candidates.trigger: SLA miss, user complaints, queueing alerts in production.
action: Step 1 - Isolate symptom: high TTFT vs high ITL (Prometheus histograms).
Step 2 - Detect saturation: monitor num_requests_running and num_requests_waiting; queue > 0 = bound.
Step 3 - Check KV health: occupancy near 100% with rising preemption -> drop --max-num-seqs or quantize KV to FP8.
Step 4 - Sequence-length correlation: ISL/OSL vs latency.
Step 5 - Distributed strategy: PCIe bottleneck? Drop TP, scale replicas instead.
operation_candidates.output: Identified bottleneck class (queue / compute / memory / interconnect) and one targeted change.

operation_candidates.name: Decide on speculative decoding
action: Draft/EAGLE-3 weights exist for target model? -> Enable; expect up to 2.5x decode speedup.
No draft model? -> Try n-gram (~1.17x, zero setup risk).
Skip if QPS is high (large batches already saturate compute -> speculation wastes capacity).
Always benchmark vs non-speculative baseline; bad hyperparameters can degrade cost-per-token by up to 175%.
operation_candidates.output: --speculative-config JSON, with measured tokens/s delta on representative traffic.

operation_candidates.name: Decide whether vLLM is the right tool at all
operation_candidates.trigger: Start of a new deployment / engine choice.
action: Production + GPU + concurrent users -> vLLM.
Dev laptop, 1 user, no concurrency -> Ollama (5-min setup).
CPU / edge / Apple Silicon production -> llama.cpp.
NVIDIA-only + 1-2 wk eng budget + max throughput -> consider TensorRT-LLM.
Heavy prefix sharing / agent state machines -> consider SGLang (RadixAttention).
